executive_brief: The Learning Platform API is a high-performance educational backend built on a fully asynchronous Python 3.12 and FastAPI stack. It implements a strict RESTful architecture under /api/v1/ with a standardized JSON envelope, utilizing PostgreSQL 16 for persistence. The system enforces a dual-role authorization model (student/instructor) and integrates the Resend SDK for transactional email notifications.

architectural_constraints: Mandatory asynchronous database access via ORM; Raw SQL is prohibited
Database tests must use real PostgreSQL instances (zero mocking)
API Versioning: /api/v1/ with consistent {data, meta, errors} envelope
Role Isolation: Instructors only manage/delete courses; Students only enroll and submit progress
Explicit review required for changes to authentication, authorization, or response envelopes

maturity_assessment: The specifications exhibit high structural integrity and technical precision. All core pillars—stack, security, and quality gates—are explicitly defined. While a minor low-severity gap exists regarding the absence of an 'Open Questions' section, the technical constraints are exhaustive enough to consider the project READY for execution.
critical_dependencies: Resend Python SDK for transactional emails
Referential integrity between Alembic migrations and schema state
Dependency between feature completion and pytest validation
